name: Syed Huzaifa Bin Afzal
headline: Cloud Infrastructure, DevOps, SRE, and Secure AI Systems
summary: Cloud infrastructure and DevOps engineer with production AWS SaaS experience and graduate work in cybersecurity, AI governance, and secure GenAI deployment. Background includes Terraform-based infrastructure, CI/CD, AWS cost optimization, monitoring, incident response, disaster recovery, and private AI platform research.

education.school: Ghulam Ishaq Khan Institute of Engineering Sciences and Technology

certifications: Foundations for Cybersecurity Analytics – University of Washington

experience.title: DevOps Engineer
highlights: Built reusable Terraform modules for AWS API Gateway, CloudFront, Route 53, OpenSearch, and Redshift to improve consistency and reduce provisioning time.
Supported production AWS SaaS infrastructure with IAM/RBAC, SSO, Systems Manager, CI/CD workflows, monitoring, and operational runbooks.
Worked on OpenSearch cross-cluster replication, Redis upgrades, disaster recovery readiness, and multi-region reliability improvements.
Used AWS Trusted Advisor, Cost Optimization Hub, reservation planning, and rightsizing to reduce cloud spend by approximately 30%.
Built S3 Inventory analytics workflows using Redshift and Superset to improve storage visibility and reporting.

experience.title: Cloud Infrastructure / Software Engineer
highlights: Automated AWS patching, scheduling, post-deployment validation, and maintenance workflows using Systems Manager, Lambda, Python, Bash, and PowerShell.
Built and maintained Jenkins CI/CD pipelines and supported CloudWatch and Datadog observability for cloud workloads.
Provisioned infrastructure for AWS EMR and ECS workloads using Terraform, Docker, ECS, ALB, Route 53, and Fargate.
Supported migration of Spark/HDP workloads from EC2 to EMR to improve scalability and operational manageability.

experience.title: Student News Reporter
highlights: Report on campus news, student life, and IT/AI developments for the Tacoma Ledger.
Interview university leaders and translate technical AI and IT topics into clear public-facing stories.
Covered UW Purple and AI community efforts across the University of Washington.

projects.name: GovernAI – Secure Enterprise GenAI Platform
highlights: Built and evaluated an on-prem GenAI platform using Ollama, Open WebUI, Docker, and Windows Server for private AI experimentation.
Designed role-based access for Standard User, AI Platform Admin, and System Administrator workflows.
Validated network behavior using host firewall rules and Wireshark and mapped governance controls to NIST AI RMF and ISO/IEC 27001:2022 concepts.
Co-authored accepted IEEE SVCC 2026 paper: 'Are You Aware of Shadow AI? GovernAI for Addressing Emerging Risks.'

projects.name: AWS Infrastructure Automation and Cost Optimization
highlights: Created reusable Terraform and automation workflows across AWS environments.
Improved cost visibility, monitoring, and operational consistency for production SaaS infrastructure.

projects.name: UW AI and Technical Storytelling
highlights: Published public-facing writing on UW Purple and campus AI adoption.
Use executive interviews and podcast conversations to explain AI, IT, and cybersecurity topics to non-specialist audiences.
